title: Klaviyo vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Klaviyo and Drip email marketing platforms. See pricing, features, and which is best for your ecommerce business.
h1: Klaviyo vs Drip: A Detailed Comparison for E-Commerce Teams
intro: Klaviyo and Drip are both powerful email marketing platforms built specifically for e-commerce businesses. Both offer deep integrations with Shopify and other platforms, SMS capabilities, and advanced automation. The key difference lies in their pricing structure, feature depth, and ideal use cases—making the choice dependent on your budget, store size, and specific marketing needs.

comparison_table.tool_b: E-commerce tracking, revenue attribution, onsite campaigns

choose_a_if: You want to start for free and scale as you grow—Klaviyo's freemium model lets you begin with up to 500 subscribers at no cost
You need predictive analytics and AI-driven insights to optimize your campaigns and customer behavior
You're building on Shopify and want the deepest native integration available in the market
choose_b_if: You're willing to pay from day one but want the lowest possible entry price at $39/month
You value a visual, intuitive automation builder and onsite campaign capabilities for better user experience
You want an affiliate program to generate recurring revenue and incentivize partners

faq.a: Klaviyo edges out Drip for most e-commerce businesses due to superior predictive analytics, a free tier for startups, and higher customer ratings (4.6 vs 4.4 on G2). However, Drip excels in revenue attribution and visual automation, making it excellent for experienced marketers prioritizing these specific features. The 'better' choice depends on your priorities and budget.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Klaviyo or Drip?
faq.a: Drip has a lower entry price at $39/month with no free tier. Klaviyo offers a free tier for up to 500 subscribers, making it cheaper for small brands just starting out. Once you exceed 500 contacts, Klaviyo's paid plans begin at $45/month, making both platforms competitively priced.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to Klaviyo?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support contact list exports and integrations with migration tools. Most subscribers, segments, and basic automation can be transferred, though you may need to rebuild complex flows manually. Both platforms have support documentation to guide the migration process.
